code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a written work into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to produce a concise phrase for each predicate.
    # When the triple shares the main subject we omit repeating it,
    # otherwise we keep the subject for clarity.
    def clause(triple: Triple, main_subject: str) -> str:
        s, p, o = triple.subject, triple.predicate, triple.object
        # Patterns that make sense when the subject is the work itself
        work_patterns = {
            "author": f"written by {o}",
            "isbnNumber": f"ISBN {o}",
            "followedBy": f"followed by {o}",
            "precededBy": f"preceded by {o}",
            "language": f"written in {o}",
            "mediaType": f"available as {o}",
            "numberOfPages": f"{o} pages",
            "country": f"published in {o}",
            "publisher": f"published by {o}",
            "firstPublicationYear": f"first published in {o}",
            "editor": f"edited by {o}",
            "impactFactor": f"impact factor {o}",
            "oclcNumber": f"OCLC number {o}",
            "libraryofCongressClassification": f"classified under {o}",
            "genre": f"a {o} work",
            "literaryGenre": f"a {o} work",
            "eissnNumber": f"e‑ISSN {o}",
            "frequency": f"issued {o}",
            "releaseDate": f"released on {o}",
        }
        # Patterns for non‑work subjects (people, organisations, etc.)
        other_patterns = {
            "almaMater": f"studied at {o}",
            "doctoralAdvisor": f"advised by {o}",
            "nationality": f"is {o}",
            "residence": f"lives in {o}",
            "codenCode": f"carries the CODEN {o}",
            "issnNumber": f"has ISSN {o}",
            "LCCN_number": f"has LCCN {o}",
            "abbreviation": f"is abbreviated as {o}",
            "academicDiscipline": f"belongs to the discipline of {o}",
            "leader": f"leader is {o}",
            "birthPlace": f"was born in {o}",
            "affiliation": f"is affiliated with {o}",
            "city": f"is located in {o}",
            "president": f"president is {o}",
            "state": f"is in the state of {o}",
            "spokenIn": f"is spoken in {o}",
            "influencedBy": f"was influenced by {o}",
            "family": f"belongs to the {o} family",
            "ethnicGroup": f"is associated with the {o} ethnic group",
            "largestCity": f"largest city is {o}",
            "location": f"is located in {o}",
            "deathPlace": f"died in {o}",
            "notableWork": f"notable work includes {o}",
            "parentCompany": f"is owned by {o}",
            "regionServed": f"serves the region of {o}",
            "founder": f"was founded by {o}",
            "capital": f"capital is {o}",
            "demonym": f"demonym is {o}",
            "leaderTitle": f"holds the title of {o}",
            "nickname": f"nicknamed {o}",
            "genus": f"belongs to the genus {o}",
        }
        if s == main_subject:
            phrase = work_patterns.get(p, f"{p} {o}")
            return phrase
        else:
            phrase = other_patterns.get(p, f"{s} {p} {o}")
            return phrase

    # Build clauses, starting with a clause that mentions the main subject
    main_subject = triples[0].subject
    main_clauses = [clause(triple) for triple in triples if triple.subject == main_subject]

    # If there are other subjects, add them as additional information
    other_clauses = [clause(triple) for triple in triples if triple.subject != main_subject]

    all_clauses = main_clauses + other_clauses

    # Join clauses into a fluent sentence
    if len(all_clauses) == 1:
        sentence = all_clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(all_clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + all_clauses[-1] + "."

    # Capitalize first letter
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
